title: visualization 1 :)
text: The visualization above is from Lab 4 where we explored graph building! I did the average life expectancy across each country across the different years, and then I did the average life expectancy in each country. The graph itself is the average minimum versus average maximum years in terms of life expectancy grouped by continents.

title: bmi 525 final vis
text:
#packages needed :)
pacman::p_load(here, dplyr, tidyverse, broom,
               gtsummary, readr, sf, sp, tmap, osmdata, ggmap, readxl,
               classInt, cowplot, maps, prettymapr, ggspatial, basemaps,
               mapview, ghibli, extrafonts, wesanderson)

Warning: package 'extrafonts' is not available for this version of R

A version of this package for your version of R might be available elsewhere,
see the ideas at
https://cran.r-project.org/doc/manuals/r-patched/R-admin.html#Installing-packages


Warning in p_install(package, character.only = TRUE, ...):


Warning in library(package, lib.loc = lib.loc, character.only = TRUE,
logical.return = TRUE, : there is no package called 'extrafonts'


Warning in pacman::p_load(here, dplyr, tidyverse, broom, gtsummary, readr, : Failed to install/load:
extrafonts



# to load my data + maps
# languages <- read_csv(here("data/languages_dataset.csv"))
world_boundaries <- st_read(here("data/World_Countries_Generalized.shp"))   

Reading layer `World_Countries_Generalized' from data source 
  `/Users/ros/Desktop/ohsu/BMI 525/quarto shiny app/data/World_Countries_Generalized.shp' 
  using driver `ESRI Shapefile'
Simple feature collection with 251 features and 4 fields
Geometry type: MULTIPOLYGON
Dimension:     XY
Bounding box:  xmin: -20037510 ymin: -30240970 xmax: 20037510 ymax: 18418390
Projected CRS: WGS 84 / Pseudo-Mercator

languages <- read_csv(here("data/22_Languages_of_the_World.csv"))

Rows: 8612 Columns: 12
── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Delimiter: ","
chr (8): id, name, macroarea, iso639p3code, countries, family_id, family, st...
dbl (3): latitude, longitude, status_code
lgl (1): is_isolate

ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

world_boundaries = world_boundaries %>%
  filter(COUNTRY != "Antarctica")

world_map = world_boundaries %>% ggplot() + geom_sf() 


#filtering the average lat and long points to plot the circles
major_families <- c("Indo-European", "Dravidian", "Austronesian",
                    "Tai-Kadai","Afro-Asiatic", "Turkic", "Sino-Tibetan",
                    "Atlantic-Congo", "Austroasiatic", "Arawakan", "Mande",
                    "Otomanguean", "Pama-Nyungan", 
                    "Nuclear Trans New Guinea",
                    "Quechuan", "Tupian")

languages_filtered <- languages %>%
  filter(!is.na(family)) %>%
  mutate(family = ifelse(family %in% major_families, family, "Other"))

family_coords <- languages %>%
  filter(family != "Other") %>%
  group_by(family) %>%
  summarise(
    lat = mean(latitude, na.rm = TRUE),
    lon = mean(longitude, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

family_counts <- languages_filtered %>%
  count(family) %>%
  left_join(family_coords, by = "family")


#creating my own color palette as I wanted ghibli but it's limited to 9 colors and I have 16 families
my_colors <- c(ghibli_palettes$YesterdayMedium[3:7], ghibli_palettes$PonyoMedium[2:6], ghibli_palettes$MarnieMedium1[3:7], wes_palette("Royal2"), wes_palette("GrandBudapest2"))

#creating the plot
world_map + 
  geom_point(data = family_counts, 
             aes(x = lon, y = lat, size = n, color = family),
             alpha = 0.7) +
  coord_sf(crs = st_crs(4326)) +
  labs(x = "Longitudinal coordinates",
       y = "Latitudinal coordinates",
       title = "Current modern languages and it's historical language family cluster") + 
  scale_color_manual(values = my_colors) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") + guides(size = "none") +
  theme(text = element_text(family = "Georgia"))

Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
(`geom_point()`).








ggsave(here("images/family_bubble_map.png"), width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range
(`geom_point()`).

saveRDS(languages, here("data/languages.rds"))

title: About me!
text: Hi! My name’s Rosalyn and I’m a first year graduate student at OHSU-PSU studying Biostatistics. In my free time I love spending time with my cats :) watch c-dramas, travel, read, and take walks.
